name: hswm-related-work-longinus-boundary
policy: Each reference records an overlap that HSWM must not claim as novel. The candidate contribution remains a hypothesis until a systematic review and the preregistered H1/H2 benchmarks support it. Absence from this registry is not evidence of priority.

candidate_contribution.claim: Lakatosian programme policy + receipt-separated authority + causal redispatch

references.title: A dynamic knowledge graph approach to distributed self-driving laboratories
references.overlap: A network of agents updates a dynamic knowledge graph while coordinating a physical closed-loop experiment.

references.title: A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery
references.overlap: A lab-in-the-loop multi-agent system generates hypotheses, proposes follow-up experiments, interprets results, and updates hypotheses.

references.title: SciAgents: Automating scientific discovery through multi-agent intelligent graph reasoning
references.overlap: Knowledge graphs and multiple language-model agents generate, critique, and refine scientific hypotheses.

references.title: AutoLabs: cognitive multi-agent systems with self-correction for autonomous chemical experimentation
references.overlap: Multiple agents plan, execute, monitor, and correct autonomous chemical experiments.

references.title: Beyond Text-Passing: Shared Cognitive Substrates for Multi-Agent LLM Coordination
references.overlap: Agents coordinate through a typed shared world model and causal graph rather than text passing alone.

references.title: Continual learning and refinement of causal models through dynamic predicate invention
references.overlap: A symbolic causal world model is learned and repaired online inside an agent decision loop.
